data.title: Mazda Foundation grants $586,361 in 2026 to 9 non-profits for food security, education, and workforce development.
data.description: In Irvine, California on February 16, 2026, the Mazda Foundation (USA), Inc. disclosed $586,361 in grant funding for 2026. The funding aims to aid nine nonprofit partners engaged in vital initiatives related to food security, education, STEM, and workforce development in the regions serviced by Mazda.

data.title: Faraday Future CEO YT Jia addresses market performance, plans for improvement in EAI Robotics and recommendation for AIxC.
data.description: LOS ANGELES, Feb 4 (BUSINESS WIRE) - Faraday Future Intelligent Electric Inc., trading as FFAI on NASDAQ, provided a business update by YT Jia, the Founder and Global Co-CEO. The California-headquartered company is a leader in shared intelligent electric mobility solutions worldwide. YT Jia expressed greetings for Chinese New Year and emphasized the importance of consistency. He highlighted significant developments over the past year, showcasing tangible progress.

data.title: Azbil wins Frost & Sullivan's 2025 APAC and Southeast Asia Company of the Year for Smart Infrastructure & Building Solutions.
data.description: Azbil Corporation has been acknowledged for its exceptional role in promoting sustainable smart infrastructure, advanced building automation, and innovation focused on people across Asia-Pacific and Southeast Asia. On Feb. 16, 2026, in San Antonio, Frost & Sullivan recognized Azbil's leadership in these areas.

data.title: Global carbon fiber market to grow from 245.37 kt in 2026 to 562.77 kt in 2031 with 18.06% CAGR, driven by APAC demand.
data.description: In Hyderabad, India, as of February 16, 2026, recent data from Mordor Intelligence indicates a significant growth projection in the global carbon fiber market. The market is expected to increase from 245.37 kilotons in 2026 to 562.77 kilotons by 2031, showcasing a notable compound annual growth rate (CAGR) of 18.06%. The demand for carbon fiber remains strong, bolstered by various factors...

data.title: MUHLENGIA wins CIPS 2025 Innovation Award for zero-electricity cooling bed to prevent pet heatstroke - Seoul, South Korea.
data.description: In Seoul, South Korea, the renowned pet brand MUHLENGIA was honored with the prestigious CIPS 2025 Innovation Award. This recognition was bestowed upon their Ultra Cooling Pet Bed, which stands out for its energy-efficient design aimed at averting pet heatstroke. This award-winning product showcases MUHLENGIA's commitment to pet comfort and safety.
